_comment: Sample history and bookmark fixtures for M05 tests.
These records reflect the extension-era field names rather than the
bookmarklet's localStorage shape, but the logical content is equivalent.
Key differences from the bookmarklet shape:
- 'favorites' renamed to 'bookmarks' in all new code
- 'downloadedAt' replaced by the 'captureStatus' field
- 'label' retained as a display hint
- 'thumbnail' remains a data URL or empty string (session cache only;
durable thumbnails live in the IndexedDB 'thumbnails' object store)
The records below cover the cases needed for M05 repository tests:
- remote-only entry (no local capture)
- entry with a thumbnail data URL
- entry that has been downloaded
- entry with LLM-fetched metadata (title + description)
- entry that failed to load (404)
- entry from a cross-origin CDN
- entry with a hex token in the URL
- entry with a query-only URL
- entry at the 30-item visible window boundary
- a set of 5 bookmark records mirroring history shape (no captureStatus)

history.title: coastal_cliffs_at_sunset_overlooking_the_ocean
history.label: photo.jpg – cdn.example.com
history._notes: Remote-only entry; LLM title set; no local bytes.

history.title: coastal_cliffs_at_sunset_detail_wave_spray
history.label: photo.jpg – cdn.example.com
history._notes: Entry with a thumbnail data URL (placeholder base64; not a real image).

history.title: coastal_cliffs_at_sunset_panoramic_view
history.label: photo.jpg – cdn.example.com
history._notes: Entry that has been downloaded; both captureStatus and downloadedAt are set.

history.title: tall_oak_trees_in_autumn_fog_morning_light
history.label: img_0001.jpg – media.example.org
history._notes: Entry with LLM-fetched title and a thumbnail.

history.title: img_0002.jpg
history.label: img_0002.jpg – media.example.org
history._notes: Entry that failed to load (404); title is URL-derived fallback; no thumbnail.

history.title: profile.jpg
history.label: profile.jpg – images.external-cdn.net
history._notes: Cross-origin CDN entry; no local capture; URL contains a numeric user ID.

history.title: 3f9a2b.jpg
history.label: 3f9a2b.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Entry with a hex token in the filename; title is URL-derived (no LLM fetch).

history.title: example.com
history.label: example.com – example.com
history._notes: Query-only URL with no meaningful path segment; title and label fall back to host.

history.title: sunlit_field_of_lavender_in_rural_france
history.label: photo.jpg – images.example.co.uk
history._notes: Subdomain and ccTLD host; thumbnail present.

history.title: 0042.jpg
history.label: 0042.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Encoded-slash path; title is URL-derived from the last path segment.

history.title: 001.jpg
history.label: 001.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Non-ASCII characters in path; URL stored as-is (percent-encoded form).

history.title: 002.jpg
history.label: 002.jpg – example.com
history._notes: URL with hash fragment; hash is preserved in stored URL.

history.title: 9007199254740993.jpg
history.label: 9007199254740993.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Large numeric value exceeding Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER in the URL.

history.title: example.com
history.label: example.com – example.com
history._notes: Query-only URL with a text parameter and a numeric ID.

history.title: thumb.jpg
history.label: thumb.jpg – example.com
history._notes: 0x-prefixed hex color value in path.

history.title: 003.jpg
history.label: 003.jpg – localhost:8080
history._notes: Localhost URL with port; tests that port is preserved in host.

history.title: 002.jpg
history.label: 002.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Space encoded as %20 in path segment.

history.title: 99.jpg
history.label: 99.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Two-digit unpadded numeric; width grows on bump past 99.

history.title: 100.jpg
history.label: 100.jpg – example.com
history._notes: Three-digit; decrement to 99 must zero-pad to preserve width.

Download record with a valid SHA-256 fingerprint (placeholder hex).
